title: gomphandra luzoniensis subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Gomphandra luzoniensis subsp. septentrionalis, also known as Philippine tree, is a plant species native to the Philippines that belongs to the family Stemonuraceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Gomphandra luzoniensis subsp. septentrionalis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
